ONCOLOGY PROGRESS NOTE
Provider: J. Chen, MD — Thoracic Medical Oncology
Encounter date: 2025-10-03
Encounter type: New patient consultation

CHIEF COMPLAINT
New diagnosis of lung cancer, here for treatment planning.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Doe is a 72-year-old gentleman referred from pulmonology after a
CT-guided core biopsy of a right upper lobe mass returned adenocarcinoma.
He reports a six-month history of dry cough and a 12-pound unintentional
weight loss. No hemoptysis. Mild dyspnea on exertion (one flight of stairs).
No bone pain, no headaches, no focal neurologic symptoms.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, COPD (GOLD stage II), former tobacco use
(45 pack-years, quit 11 years ago).

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
As above; otherwise non-contributory on a 12-system review.

PHYSICAL EXAM
Vitals stable. Lungs with mildly diminished breath sounds at the right apex,
no rales or wheezes. No supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Cardiac exam
unremarkable. No hepatomegaly. Neuro grossly intact.

IMAGING REVIEW
CT chest 2025-09-08: 3.2 cm spiculated right upper lobe mass, right hilar
adenopathy (largest node 1.4 cm), no contralateral disease, no pleural
effusion.
PET-CT 2025-09-22: FDG-avid right upper lobe mass (SUVmax 11.2),
FDG-avid right hilar node (SUVmax 6.8). No distant metastatic disease.
MRI brain 2025-09-25: no intracranial metastatic disease.

PATHOLOGY REVIEW
Outside core biopsy reviewed in our institutional tumor board on 2025-10-01.
Confirmed adenocarcinoma of the lung. Molecular pending.

ASSESSMENT
1. Stage IIIA (cT2aN2M0) non-small-cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma subtype
   — primary diagnosis. Patient has lung cancer and is appropriate for
   concurrent chemoradiation followed by consolidation immunotherapy
   pending molecular results.
2. COPD — stable, continue tiotropium.
3. Hypertension — stable.

PLAN
- Multidisciplinary tumor board discussion completed; recommend definitive
  concurrent chemoradiotherapy.
- Carboplatin / paclitaxel weekly with concurrent thoracic radiotherapy
  (60 Gy / 30 fx), starting on receipt of insurance authorization.
- Hold consolidation durvalumab decision until EGFR / ALK results return.
- Smoking cessation counseling reinforced (patient remains abstinent).
- Follow-up in two weeks or sooner if symptoms.

J. Chen, MD — electronically signed 2025-10-03 14:18
